commit.message:
Release WebGate FW3.7

New features:
- SSH backdoor via RCE CVE with Scope Agreement (typed 'I accept')
- TOR routing via bridge (obfs4/webtunnel/snowflake)
- SQLMap interactive dump dialog: pick DB -> table -> dump in GUI
- CVEExploitOfferDialog: auto-prompt when RCE CVE detected after scan
- ScopeAgreementDialog: typed 'I accept' confirmation before exploit
- SSHBackdoorDialog: configure SSH credentials + TOR bridge
- SQLMapDumpDialog: GUI-based DB/table selector with background dump

Improvements:
- Simplified reports: SQL injection Y/N + vulnerable param/payload
- XSStrike output simplified to injection Y/N + vulnerable URL
- Progress bar with gradient, % overlay, bright pulse edge animation
- Logo glitch animation (random interval glitch effect)
- Particle burst animation on scan start
- FW badge in logo canvas
- Scan-line border animation during active scan

Fixes:
- Removed COPY button (caused GUI freeze/lag on Wayland)
- Version strings updated to FW3.7
- LICENSE added (authorized use only)
- README.md fully rewritten with all features documented

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(sync): full-sync returns same-device docs when since=0

When since="0" the client has an empty local DB (fresh install or
post-logout). The device_id != requester filter was incorrectly
excluding documents that the same device had created previously,
causing those docs to be invisible after logout+re-login.

Now when since=="0", the device_id filter is skipped so all of the
user's records are returned unconditionally.

commit.message:
feat: implement timepass programming language v0.1.0

- Lexer: full tokenizer with keywords, operators, string/number literals
- Parser: recursive descent parser producing a typed AST
- Interpreter: tree-walking evaluator with lexical scoping
- Objects: int, float, str, bool, null, list, map, function, struct, channel
- Environment: let (immutable) / var (mutable) with closure support
- Builtins: 40+ built-in functions (I/O, math, string, collections, time)
- Goroutines: go { } syntax backed by real Go goroutines
- Channels: chan(), send (<-), receive (<-) for concurrent communication
- REPL: interactive shell with multi-line input and colour errors
- Examples: hello, fibonacci, structs, closures, concurrent, algorithms

fix(ingest): enforce title-content consistency in case extraction prompt

LLM was generating problem_title from surrounding chunk context instead
of the actual case content, causing title/problem/solution mismatches.
Added explicit rules: title must match problem_summary, and each case
must be self-consistent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Release v1.0.1: hardened core, FFI/Swift surface complete, full docs

Protocol:
- mandatory authorization artifact for external group join
- strict identity binding in handshake (closes misbinding vector)
- franking policy enforced on receive path, not only sender
- hardened group default: shielded posture out-of-the-box
- group encrypt/decrypt: Edit, Delete, encrypt_with_policy content types
- epp_group_decrypt_ex: full EppGroupDecryptResult (sealed/franking/message-id)
- deterministic epp_group_compute_message_id (group_id+epoch+leaf+gen)
- epp_group_authorize_external_join: explicit auth artifact flow
- session identity getters: get_id, get_identity_binding_hash, get_peer/local_identity
- OTK replenishment: epp_prekey_bundle_replenish + IIdentityEventHandler
- event callbacks: C FFI handlers for session, group, identity events
- envelope metadata: epp_envelope_metadata_parse / _free

C API:
- separate epp_client_api.h / epp_common_api.h headers
- epp_group_decrypt_result_free safety note: zero-init requirement documented
- epp_group_join_external signature: added authorization params

Swift wrapper:
- EppSession: get_id, identityBindingHash, peerIdentity, localIdentity
- EppHandshake: finishVerifyingPeer defensive peer verification
- EppGroupSession: encrypt_edit, encrypt_delete, decrypt_ex, compute_message_id
- EppIdentity: set_event_handler, replenish_one_time_pre_keys
- EppHandshakeResponder: public Swift class (parallel to EppHandshakeInitiator)

Docs:
- ffi-api.md: 20 missing functions documented (decrypt_ex, edit/delete,
  authorize_external_join, session identity, OTK replenish, event callbacks,
  envelope metadata, epp_group_compute_message_id, content type table)
- ffi-swift.md: corrected EppHandshakeResponder (was "C FFI only")
- docs/swift-session-verification.md: verified handshake UX flow
- docs/client-production-checklist.md, server-production-checklist.md,
  protocol-release-checklist.md: production gate checklists
- docs/release-v1.0.1.md: release notes and artifact checksums

Tests:
- 245 tests pass (ffi_test, api_test, integration_test, attack_poc)
- full FFI surface coverage: callbacks, OTK replenish, ReInit commit API

commit.message:
fix: full-sync fallback when Room is empty + correct doc/node upsert order

Three bugs fixed:
1. DocumentListViewModel and SyncWorker now check countDocuments() before
   using the stored lastSyncHlc cursor. If Room is empty (e.g. reinstall
   with DataStore backup restored), since is forced to "0" so all server
   documents are re-downloaded regardless of stale HLC cursor.
2. Node upsert now happens AFTER document upsert in both DocumentListViewModel
   and SyncWorker (was reversed, risking application-level ordering issues).
3. Added DocumentDao.countDocuments() query used by the empty-DB detection.

Root cause of missing docs: Android backs up DataStore (device_id + lastSyncHlc)
but not Room DB. After reinstall, the stale HLC cursor caused the server pull
to return only changes newer than the old cursor, silently skipping docs that
were already synced before the reinstall.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: preserve Secret field during BaseAsset.Visit() merge (#213)

Visit() unconditionally overwrote the existing Secret with the incoming
asset's Secret, even when it was nil or empty. This caused capabilities
(e.g. Constantine) that emit updated assets without the Secret field to
destroy the credential reference stored in the database, breaking all
subsequent jobs that depend on it.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: XDP host-inbound-all drops unknown services, causing cluster split-brain

xdp_policy.c host-inbound fallback checked `hif != 0 && (flags & hif)`
but host_inbound_flag() returns 0 for unknown services (e.g. heartbeat
UDP 4784). When host_inbound_flags == HOST_INBOUND_ALL, this incorrectly
denied the packet. xdp_forward.c already had the correct check
(`flags != HOST_INBOUND_ALL`).

After `bpfrxd cleanup` during deploy, conntrack sessions are wiped.
New heartbeat packets hit xdp_policy as new flows, got denied by the
broken check, and both nodes declared peer lost — split-brain.

Also fixes isPeerInterface() to use FPC slot→node-id mapping for
cross-node monitor interface proxy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(hmr): name-based signal state preservation [#721] (#925)

* feat(hmr): name-based signal state preservation during HMR [#721]

Replace position-based signal matching with name-based matching for HMR
state preservation. The compiler now emits `signal(init, 'varName')`,
and the HMR runtime matches old→new signals by key instead of array
index. This means inserting, deleting, or reordering `let` declarations
no longer resets all component signal state.

Unnamed signals (from query()/form() internals) fall back to
position-based matching.

Closes #721

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(hmr): name-based signal state preservation during HMR [#721]

Replace position-based signal matching with name-based matching for HMR
state preservation. The compiler now emits `signal(init, 'varName')`,
and the HMR runtime matches old→new signals by key instead of array
index. This means inserting, deleting, or reordering `let` declarations
no longer resets all component signal state.

Unnamed signals (from query()/form() internals) fall back to
position-based matching.

Closes #721

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui-server): wrap V2 factories with wrapFactory in HMR tests [#721]

Name-based signal tests used bare factories for V2, so during
__$refreshPerform the refreshSignals stash was never populated.
Wrap V2 factories with wrapFactory to match real codegen behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(hmr): robust signal collection and mount guard for Bun HMR [#721]

Three fixes for HMR signal state preservation in Bun's dev bundler:

1. globalThis signal collector stack — Bun creates separate module
   instances for @vertz/ui/internals (FR runtime) and @vertz/ui
   (component code). The module-scoped signalCollectorStack was
   different in each instance, causing zero signals to be collected
   during HMR factory re-execution. Fixed by persisting the stack on
   globalThis via Symbol.for('vertz:signal-collector-stack').

2. mount() HMR guard — Bun re-evaluates ALL modules on initial load
   (double evaluation), re-running mount() and creating duplicate
   component instances. Added a globalThis-persisted WeakMap guard
   that returns the existing handle on re-mount.

3. Content hash change detection — __$refreshReg now takes a content
   hash parameter. When Bun re-evaluates unchanged modules in a chunk,
   hash comparison prevents cascading re-mounts. Only the actually-
   changed module gets marked dirty.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat(cli): implement CLI skeleton with entry/run-main separation

Separate the CLI entry point into two layers following openclaw's architecture:
- bin.mjs: npm bin wrapper that imports dist/entry.js after build
- src/entry.ts: process entry point with dynamic import of run-main
- src/cli/run-main.ts: runCli() function that delegates to Commander parseAsync
- src/commands/config.ts: stub `config get <key>` command for step 2 checklist

This structure mirrors openclaw's bin → entry → run-main → program → commands
flow, enabling clean separation between the npm binary, process initialization,
and CLI command routing.

commit.message:
Add Markdown parser and highlighter in languages-markup/

New languages-markup/ directory for markup languages (markdown, and
later noweb, html, tex).

The parser is a simple recursive-descent/line-oriented lexer that
produces a proper AST (headings, fenced code, bold/italic, links,
images, lists, blockquotes) rather than a fuzzy AST.

commit.message:
fix(desktop): properly extract and render QR SVG with explicit size and white background

Signal's QR SVG has viewBox 0 0 16 16 with no explicit size and no
background. Add width=800 height=800 and a white rect background
before converting to PNG.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: sync readiness reset + hold timer election wakeup (#133, #134)

#133: Reset syncReady=false on total peer disconnect (both fabrics down).
Re-set true only after fresh BulkEnd. Individual fabric failover
(fab0→fab1) does not reset readiness.

#134: Schedule time.AfterFunc at ReadySince + takeoverHoldTime to
re-run election. Cancel on readiness loss. Prevents indefinite
secondary state when no other events trigger re-evaluation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: require ALL VRRP instances MASTER for RG activation (#132)

rg_active was set as soon as any single VRRP instance in the RG became
MASTER, exposing partially-transitioned ownership during failover when
one interface reaches MASTER before others.

Change reconcileLocked() from anyMasterLocked() to allMasterLocked()
so the state machine requires every VRRP instance in the RG to be
MASTER before activating. Gate watchVRRPEvents() side-effects
(rg_active, blackhole removal, services) on tr.Changed && tr.Active
so they only fire on the final activation transition.

The dual-inactive window fix is preserved: Secondary still defers
rg_active=false until VRRP BACKUP event (deactivation path unchanged).

commit.message:
Fix pre-commit hook to re-stage formatted files, slim bench tests

- Pre-commit hook now re-adds staged files after formatting so the
  committed content includes formatting fixes
- Benchmark tests reduced to single-pass smoke checks (0.08s vs 11s)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: sync established session state on periodic sweep (#131)

The sweep only replicated sessions at creation time (Created >=
lastSweepTime), leaving established flows with stale TCP state,
timeouts, and last-seen timestamps on standby.

Broaden the filter to also include sessions with LastSeen >=
lastSweepTime, so any session with recent packet activity gets
re-synced. The receiver already overwrites existing sessions, so
no wire format changes are needed.

Remove the GLOBAL_CTR_SESSIONS_NEW fast-path skip since it only
tracked new session creation, not ongoing activity.

fix: IPVLAN overlay reconciliation + dual-fabric auto-detect (#127-#130)

#127: Reconcile IPVLAN addresses on reapply — add missing, remove stale
addresses instead of skipping when overlay already exists on correct parent.

#128: Clean up stale fab0/fab1 overlays during config transitions —
inline cleanup in applyConfig removes interfaces not in active set.

#129: Probe ARP/NDP on overlay interface (fab0/fab1), not parent —
neighbor learning uses overlay ifindex, FabricFwdInfo uses parent ifindex
for XDP redirect. monitorFabricState watches overlay link state too.

#130: Auto-detect both FabricInterface and Fabric1Interface from fab0/fab1
syntax. Derive Fabric1PeerAddress from /30 or /31 point-to-point CIDR.

Instead of screenshotting Signal Desktop's QR (which gets destroyed by

linking URL from the DOM via DevTools and regenerate a clean QR code

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(agent): sistema de cola para CSF/Opinión de todos los clientes

- loadRfcsFromFiel(): detecta todos los RFCs con FIEL completa en fiel/
- runScraperForRfc(rfc): ejecuta scraper_sat.js y detecta éxito por stdout
- processQueue(): procesa la cola secuencialmente (un browser a la vez)
  - Cada RFC tiene hasta 3 intentos externos (outer attempts)
  - Si falla → al final de la cola; si agota 3 intentos → se descarta
  - Los 3 intentos internos de CSF y 32-D ya estaban en scraper_sat.js
- POST /enqueue-all: carga todos los RFCs e inicia la cola
- GET  /queue-status: devuelve estado actual (running, pending, queue)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(ai/gemini): Vertex AI com SSO organizacional configurável na UI de AI Settings

Adiciona suporte completo ao Gemini Vertex AI com autenticação via
Application Default Credentials (gcloud SSO), configurável diretamente
na aba AI Settings da aplicação — igual aos outros providers.

Backend:
- storage/user_tokens_store.go: novos campos gemini_auth_mode,
  gemini_vertex_project, gemini_vertex_location + migration SQLite
- handlers/ai_tokens.go: salva/retorna config Vertex, valida conexão
  via gcloud auth print-access-token (provider 'gemini-vertex')
- handlers/ai_diagnostics.go: usa vertex quando gemini_auth_mode=vertex
- ai/gemini_provider.go: modo vertex usa Bearer token via gcloud +
  endpoint aiplatform.googleapis.com (Vertex AI)
- ai/config.go: campos GeminiAuthMode, GeminiVertexProject, GeminiVertexLocation

Frontend (AISettingsTab):
- Select "Modo de Autenticação": API Key ou Vertex AI (SSO)
- Painel Vertex: campo Projeto GCP + select de região + botão Testar Conexão
- Testar Conexão valida gcloud auth print-access-token em tempo real
- Persiste preferência junto com os demais tokens do usuário

Uso (após configurar na UI):
  gcloud auth application-default login
  Selecionar provider "Gemini" > modo "Vertex AI - SSO" > inserir projeto GCP
